title: Cross-session dome-sync-clone sweep is structurally impossible — retire the misleading 2026-05-29 sweep, strengthen same-session reuse-vs-reclone logic, document operator-side fallback.
author: tinker
source_directive: DIRECTIVE-20260531-003 (operator filed MEDIUM at 05:22Z after manually cleaning 3 stale /sessions/<id>/dome-sync-clone directories aged 10h/12h/15h. All three were created AFTER the 2026-05-29 cross-session sweep landed in FUSE, so the sweep is not working.)
executive_summary: Hypothesis (a) from the directive is CONFIRMED. The cross-session sweep added 2026-05-29 (workspace-sync.md line 77, `find /sessions -maxdepth 2 -type d -name 'dome-sync-clone' -mmin +60 ! -path "$CLONE" -exec rm -rf {} \; 2>/dev/null || true`) cannot ever delete a foreign session's dome-sync-clone because (1) /sessions/<other-id>/ is owned by nobody:nogroup mode 750 and the cowork-Haiku agent's per-session UID has no group membership granting read or traverse access, and (2) the `2>/dev/null` swallows every Permission-denied error so the find walk silently returns zero matches. The directive's Option (ii) — moving CLONE to /tmp/dome-sync-clone — was investigated and ALSO confirmed unworkable: /tmp is mode 1777 (sticky) and cross-session UID namespace mapping shows foreign files as nobody:nogroup; `rm` of foreign-owned /tmp files fails with `Operation not permitted` (verified by direct test). The correct disposition is (i) + (iv) hybrid: retire the misleading sweep, strengthen same-session abandonment recovery (which IS within the agent's perms), and document operator-side fallback for cross-session orphans (which is structurally outside any single agent's perms).

step_1_hypothesis_a_confirmed.session_under_test: wonderful-epic-hypatia (uid=4419, gid=4419, no group memberships beyond own)
step_1_hypothesis_a_confirmed.ls_foreign_session_root: FAILS — `ls: cannot open directory '/sessions/admiring-adoring-volta/': Permission denied` (exit 0 from ls because flag-default; underlying syscall failure swallowed by globbing).
step_1_hypothesis_a_confirmed.find_sweep_actual_output_no_quiet: Stream of `find: '/sessions/<id>': Permission denied` for ~3300 entries. find itself walks /sessions/ at depth 1 successfully (the top-level read of /sessions/ works), but cannot descend into any /sessions/<other-id>/ directory.
step_1_hypothesis_a_confirmed.find_sweep_with_quiet: Returns 0 paths (Permission denied lost to 2>/dev/null). Exit 0 — looks identical to 'no orphans existed'. Indistinguishable from working.
verification_commands_run_at_2026_05_31T: ls -la /sessions/ | head -5  # drwxr-xr-x 3333 nobody nogroup, then drwxr-x--- per-session-dir entries
find /sessions -maxdepth 2 -type d -name 'dome-sync-clone' 2>&1 | head  # find: '/sessions/<id>': Permission denied (×~3300)

step_2_option_ii_also_unworkable.summary: Moving CLONE to /tmp/dome-sync-clone does NOT make the orphan sweep cross-session-capable. /tmp is shared across sessions but ownership is namespace-mapped: foreign-session files show as nobody:nogroup to my session view, and the sticky bit on /tmp prevents `rm` of files I do not own.
step_2_option_ii_also_unworkable.verification: Direct test against /tmp/clean_clone.txt (a marker file owned by `nobody:nogroup`, originally written 2026-05-30 by /sessions/bold-vigilant-maxwell/dome-review-clean). `rm /tmp/clean_clone.txt` → `Operation not permitted` (exit 1). The file was restored from a captured copy after the test.
step_2_option_ii_also_unworkable.implication: Option (ii) trades 'find sees zero paths' for 'find finds the orphan but rm fails'. Neither helps reclaim disk; the difference is purely cosmetic. /sessions/<id>/ is actually marginally better for sandbox isolation because foreign sessions can't even enumerate the orphan name. Recommend keeping CLONE at /sessions/<id>/dome-sync-clone.

step_2_option_iii_out_of_scope.summary: Operator-side scheduled task with elevated perms — directive's non-goal #1 explicitly forbids ('Do NOT add a system-level cron job or root-equivalent process — out of scope for the operator's cowork environment').

step_2_option_iv_is_already_the_status_q.summary: Manual operator cleanup — the directive notes operator-side cost is ~30 sec per cleanup × every few days = 1-2 min/week. The operator IS doing this and the disk has never run out. The TRUE concern (per the directive's priority_note) is the misleading appearance of automation: the 2026-05-29 sweep is FUSE-deployed and reads as if it works, but produces 0-clean every cycle. Future operator-direct fixes that assume cross-session sweep capability will fail the same way.

step_2_option_i_is_within_agent_perms_an.summary: Same-session orphan recovery — the workspace-sync agent CAN clean orphans within its own session UID (no perms issue). Currently the agent reuses any existing $CLONE on subsequent runs (workspace-sync.md line 83 `if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then ... clone fresh ...`) which is correct for normal cases. But if a prior run died mid-cycle and left a corrupted/stale clone, the reuse path may carry forward bad state. Adding a 'force re-clone if existing $CLONE has mtime >2h old' check converts orphan-from-abandoned-cycle into clean re-clone on the next hourly run, without affecting normal flow.

why_orphans_arise_in_the_first_place.exit_trap_scope_limitation: workspace-sync.md line 141 sets `trap 'rm -rf "$CLONE" 2>/dev/null' EXIT INT TERM` inside Step 1's bash block. Under the MCP bash tool model, EACH bash call is a fresh subshell — the trap fires at the end of Step 1's bash session (which would actively HARM the cycle if it deleted the clone before Step 2 ran; in practice the trap definition is lost when Step 1's bash exits normally so the EXIT side-effect does not actually fire across calls). Step 2-5 bash calls have no trap. The final explicit `rm -rf "${CLONE}"` at line 1367 only runs if the LLM reaches the Cleanup section. Common abandonment paths (LLM context exhausted, tool error mid-cycle, scheduled-task timeout, host session reset) skip the Cleanup section and orphan the clone.
why_orphans_arise_in_the_first_place.cross_session_consequence: When the cowork session itself ends (host process death, LLM-agent VM reset, scheduled-task host change), any orphaned /sessions/<dead-id>/dome-sync-clone persists with the prior session's UID. No future session can read or remove it (perms). It accumulates until operator cleanup.
why_orphans_arise_in_the_first_place.rate_estimate: Operator-observed 3 orphans aged 10-15h after the 2026-05-29 fix bootstrap into FUSE (operator manual cleanup 2026-05-31~05:00Z). That implies roughly 3 session-deaths × 90MB = 270MB accumulated over a ~24h window. Annualized: ~100GB/year if left unmanaged. At current operator cadence (~1 cleanup/2-3 days), the steady-state is bounded at ~200-400MB.

recommendation.rationale: Option (i) is the only mechanism within the agent's perms. Option (iv) is the only mechanism for cross-session. Combining them honestly bounds the problem AND removes the false signal that the 2026-05-29 cross-session sweep produces.
recommendation.why_not_just_i: (i) alone leaves cross-session orphans unaddressed. The operator IS already running (iv) ad-hoc; document it so the cost stays bounded and future tinker/integrity runs do not redundantly propose 'add cross-session cleanup' (this is the third proposal in 6 days on the topic — the recurring confusion is itself a tax).
recommendation.why_not_just_iv: (iv) alone leaves the misleading 2026-05-29 sweep in workspace-sync.md, where every future tinker/agent reading the prompt will think automated cross-session cleanup exists. Worse, the next time a cross-session-orphan issue arises, operator-Claude will look at the sweep, see it's already there, and (correctly) conclude it isn't working — repeating this entire diagnostic. Retire the broken code so future readers see honest state.

patches.where: Lines 67-78 — the cross-session orphan sweep block ending with `find /sessions -maxdepth 2 -type d -name 'dome-sync-clone' -mmin +60 ! -path "$CLONE" -exec rm -rf {} \; 2>/dev/null || true`
patches.replacement_text_summary: Replace with a short explanatory comment block documenting WHY cross-session sweep is structurally impossible and pointing to the operator-side cleanup pattern.
patches.replacement_text:
# Cross-session orphan handling (PROP-067, 2026-05-31): NOT automated.
# The 2026-05-29 attempt at `find /sessions -maxdepth 2 -type d -name 'dome-sync-clone' ...`
# was structurally impossible — /sessions/<other-id>/ is owned by nobody:nogroup
# mode 750 and this agent's per-session UID has no group access; every find walk
# of a foreign session returned Permission denied (silently swallowed by
# `2>/dev/null`). Moving CLONE to /tmp does not help either: /tmp is sticky-bit
# 1777 with cross-session UID namespace mapping; `rm` of foreign-owned /tmp
# files fails with Operation not permitted. See PROP-067 for the full
# diagnostic. Cross-session orphans are cleaned by the operator from a shell
# with cross-session read perms — see the 'Operator-side cleanup' subsection
# in CLAUDE.md's Two-Repo Architecture for the documented command.

patches.where: After line 65 (the existing /tmp/ws-sync-* /tmp/dome-sync-* intra-session sweep), before the `if [ ! -d "$CLONE" ]; then` clone-fresh guard at line 83
patches.insertion_text:
# Same-session stale-clone recovery (PROP-067, 2026-05-31). If $CLONE
# exists from a prior cycle in THIS session AND its mtime is more than 2 hours
# old, the prior cycle abandoned the clone (LLM context exhaustion, tool error,
# scheduled-task timeout). Reusing it could carry forward stale .git state.
# Force a fresh re-clone instead. The 2-hour threshold protects against
# rapid-fire same-session reruns (hourly cadence + clock skew). Within the
# session's UID this `rm -rf` succeeds (no perms barrier).
if [ -d "$CLONE" ]; then
  CLONE_MTIME=$(stat -c %Y "$CLONE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  NOW_TS=$(date -u +%s)
  CLONE_AGE_S=$(( NOW_TS - CLONE_MTIME ))
  if [ "$CLONE_AGE_S" -gt 7200 ]; then
    echo "[PROP-067] Stale same-session clone detected (age $((CLONE_AGE_S/60))min > 120min). Forcing re-clone."
    rm -rf "$CLONE"
  fi
fi

patches.where: Inside `## Architecture` / `### Two-Repo Architecture (Filesystem Constraint)`, after the existing 'Operator-side Git Data API escape hatch' and 'Operator-side `allow_cowork_file_delete`' subsections (both already documented as operator-side capabilities).
patches.insertion_text: **Operator-side cross-session dome-sync-clone cleanup (PROP-067, 2026-05-31):** When the operator notices stale `/sessions/*/dome-sync-clone` directories accumulating (typically 1-3 every few days, ~90MB each), clean them by hand from the host shell. The cowork agent cannot do this itself — `/sessions/<id>/` is owned by `nobody:nogroup` mode 750 and per-session UIDs have no cross-session read perms, so `workspace-sync`'s `find /sessions ...` walk silently returns zero paths (PROP-067 diagnostic). The operator's shell has owner-equivalent perms via the cowork sandbox host mount and CAN see and remove these orphans. Recommended one-liner: `for d in /sessions/*/dome-sync-clone; do [ -d "$d" ] && [ $(($(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y "$d"))) -gt 21600 ] && echo "removing $d (age $(( ($(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y "$d"))/3600 ))h)" && rm -rf "$d"; done` (only deletes clones older than 6 hours to avoid concurrent-active sessions). Frequency: ~1-2 minutes per week of operator time. Disk pressure does not require this — workspace-sync's pre-flight `df -m /sessions` check will refuse to clone if free space is tight, so failure mode is benign (cycle skips, no data loss); the operator-side cleanup is purely to keep `/sessions/` disk footprint bounded.

non_patches_considered_and_rejected.reason_rejected: Confirmed unworkable in diagnostic Step 2 — /tmp is sticky-bit 1777 with cross-session UID namespace mapping; foreign-owned /tmp files cannot be `rm`'d by this agent's UID. Trades silent-zero-paths for silent-rm-permission-denied. No improvement.

non_patches_considered_and_rejected.option: (iii) Operator-side scheduled task with elevated perms
non_patches_considered_and_rejected.reason_rejected: Out of scope per directive non-goal #1 — 'Do NOT add a system-level cron job or root-equivalent process'.

non_patches_considered_and_rejected.option: Strengthen the EXIT trap to cover Step 2-5 bash calls
non_patches_considered_and_rejected.reason_rejected: Structurally impossible under MCP bash tool model. Each bash call is a fresh subshell; traps set in one call's process do NOT survive to subsequent calls. The reliable end-of-procedure cleanup is the final explicit `rm -rf` at line 1367, which already exists and runs when the LLM reaches it. The unfixable failure mode is the LLM abandoning the cycle before reaching that line — no in-prompt mechanism can guarantee against agent context exhaustion or tool errors. This is structural, not a coding gap.

non_patches_considered_and_rejected.option: Atomic-mtime touch at end of each step
non_patches_considered_and_rejected.reason_rejected: Would help the proposed P2 same-session age check be more accurate (currently the age check fires on cycles aborted >2h before, which is fine), but adds prompt complexity without solving the cross-session problem. Skip.

file_ownership_impact.build_js_OWNERSHIP_changes: NONE. Patches modify only `monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md` (already classified 'git'-owned under build.js OWNERSHIP and listed in workspace-sync's NEVER_PUSH) and `CLAUDE.md` (already 'git'-owned). No new files introduced. No file's ownership classification changes.
file_ownership_impact.workspace_sync_md_NEVER_PUSH_changes: NONE. workspace-sync.md remains in NEVER_PUSH; CLAUDE.md remains in NEVER_PUSH (both already listed).
file_ownership_impact.CLAUDE_md_File_Ownership_Rules_touchpoin: NONE in the Phase-1 ownership table. The new operator-side cleanup subsection (P3) is documentation under Architecture/Two-Repo Architecture, parallel to the existing 'Git Data API escape hatch' and `allow_cowork_file_delete` subsections. Both of those are also operator-only capabilities documented in the same section; this fits the same pattern.

verification_pattern.post_apply_check_1: Confirm patches landed: `grep -n 'PROP-067' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md CLAUDE.md` should show at least 3 hits.
verification_pattern.post_apply_check_2: Confirm cross-session sweep removed: `grep -n 'find /sessions -maxdepth 2' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md` should return zero matches.
verification_pattern.post_apply_check_3: Confirm same-session stale-clone check present: `grep -A 5 'Stale same-session clone' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md` should show the new block.
verification_pattern.post_apply_check_4: Confirm operator-cleanup subsection present in CLAUDE.md: `grep -n 'Operator-side cross-session dome-sync-clone cleanup' CLAUDE.md` should return one hit.
verification_pattern.runtime_verification: Watch the next ~24h of workspace-sync run reports — agent_notes should NOT mention 'cross-session sweep' or 'find /sessions'. Operator should continue to observe cross-session orphans (the fix does not stop them from accumulating; it only retires the misleading sweep code). Operator's manual cleanup cadence should remain unchanged or improve slightly because the new CLAUDE.md subsection gives them a copy-pasteable one-liner.

estimated_effort: Decider self-apply, 15-20 min Opus. Two prompt edits + one CLAUDE.md insertion. No new scripts.

risks.risk: P2 same-session stale-clone check fires too aggressively and forces re-clone during normal slow cycles (e.g., if a cycle just took 2h+ to complete legitimately).
risks.mitigation: 2h threshold is conservative — typical workspace-sync cycles are 1-5 min. The mtime check is on the clone DIRECTORY (updated whenever any file inside is created/touched), so a still-active cycle keeps the mtime fresh. The threshold could be lowered to 90min if real-world cycles never exceed it (verifiable from workspace-sync run report durations).

risks.risk: Removing the cross-session sweep code reduces the apparent surface of 'we tried to fix this' and may make the operator think nothing is being done.
risks.mitigation: The replacement comment block in P1 explicitly documents WHY the sweep is impossible and points to CLAUDE.md's operator-side subsection. Future agents reading the prompt will see honest state, not absent state. This is a feature, not a risk.

risks.risk: Operator finds the manual one-liner annoying and would prefer a more automated approach via a different mechanism not yet considered.
risks.mitigation: PROP-067 explicitly recommends (iv) status quo augmented by (i). If operator wants a more sophisticated solution (e.g., a host-side shell snippet bound to a desktop-app shortcut), that's a separate operator-tool conversation outside the cowork-agent perms model. Document the limit in CLAUDE.md and revisit only if the cleanup tax becomes burdensome.

related_proposals.relation: Original workspace-sync disaster fix that added the clone-hygiene workstream B. The 2026-05-29 cross-session sweep was a PROP-051 follow-up. PROP-067 retires that specific follow-up without disturbing the rest of PROP-051.
PROP-061 Step 4c fix. Independent of PROP-067 — PROP-064 deals with git→FUSE direction; PROP-067 deals with clone-lifecycle disk hygiene.
Step 4c helper script extraction (authored same morning). Independent — different subsystem.

directive_disposition.status_recommendation: completed (this PROP authoring is the requested expected_output: PROP file + diagnostic confirmation + recommendation + patch)
directive_disposition.follow_up_for_operator: Review PROP-067 patches. If approved, the decider can self-apply (all three patches are mechanical text edits on git-owned files in NEVER_PUSH). If approved with changes, file a PROP-067 amendment. The directive's status field should be marked 'completed' after this run's report is written.

superseded_reason: Operator feedback (DIRECTIVE-20260531-005): an agent should always be able to clean up its own clones. PROP-067 misframes the problem around cross-session sweep impossibility (real but irrelevant); the actual problem is that workspace-sync (and possibly other agents) leak their own clones because the Cleanup section at end-of-run is skip-by-omission vulnerable — same root cause as PROP-066 Step 4c silent-skip. PROP-068 will address: (a) unconditional pre-cycle blow-away (each cycle deletes any pre-existing  as its first action, no threshold), (b) audit of all 13 scheduled agents for the same leak pattern, (c) operator-side cleanup demoted from regular maintenance to bug-detection only. The diagnostic content of PROP-067 (perms barrier proof for /sessions and /tmp) remains valuable historical record and stays in the file unchanged.
